name: Replit Agent
description: Autonomous in-browser/cloud agent that builds, tests, and deploys apps from natural-language prompts inside Replit.

first-release.comment: Replit Agent first launched September 2024; subsequent versions v2 (Feb 2025), Agent 3 (Sep 2025), Agent 4 (Mar 2026). Checked 2026-06-05.

status.comment: Generally available; Agent 4 is the current generation as of mid-2026.

license.comment: Closed-source SaaS; free Starter tier plus paid plans.

specialization.comment: Broad app-building agent; also handles mobile (React Native + Expo) but positioned as general full-stack app builder.

interface.comment: Runs in the browser-based Replit workspace; also available via iPhone app (May 2026). No standalone desktop IDE or CLI for the Agent itself.

host-ides.comment: Operates inside Replit's cloud workspace in the browser (and mobile app).

open-source.comment: Replit Agent is a proprietary, closed-source product.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: A hosted cloud development service; provisions databases, runs builds and deploys without the user leaving the browser tab.

default-model-provider.comment: Powered by Anthropic Claude since 2024; Agent 4 reportedly uses Sonnet for sustained work and Opus for complex refactoring. Users do not pick the agent's driving model.

model-choice.comment: The agent's own model is fixed (Claude). Replit AI Integrations lets the *built app* call OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/OpenRouter, but that is for the user's app, not switching the agent's driver model.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Agent runs on Replit's billing/credits; no documented option to supply your own API key for the agent itself.

local-model-support.comment: Cloud-only hosted service; no local/self-hosted model support.

multi-file-editing.comment: Scaffolds and edits full multi-file projects; Agent 4 does complex multi-file refactoring.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Plans, scaffolds, writes code, installs dependencies, tests, fixes errors in a reflection loop, and deploys end-to-end from a prompt.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Installs dependencies, runs builds and tests as part of its loop inside the workspace.

mcp-support.comment: Replit Agent supports third-party connectors (Stripe, OpenAI, etc.); a community/Replit MCP server exists to control Replit workspaces externally, but Replit does not clearly document the Agent itself consuming MCP servers as tools. Marked null pending primary confirmation (checked 2026-06-05).

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Agent 4 runs parallel agents simultaneously on a single project; the agent can also build other agents and workflows.

codebase-indexing.comment: No clear documentation of a built semantic embeddings index of the repo (vs reading files on demand). Marked null rather than guessing.

planning-mode.comment: Agent plans the work and offers an Extended Thinking mode for complex tasks before building.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts image/design input (Figma and other design-tool imports into Agent).

browser-computer-use.comment: Agent periodically tests the app it builds using a browser, generates a report, and fixes the issues it finds.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Per Replit's Agent 4 blog, the agent itself splits a single large task into smaller pieces and works on them simultaneously with sub-agents, and invokes specialized sub-agents to resolve merge conflicts - model-driven delegation, not user-launched parallel runs. Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Conversational prompts drive an autonomous build loop; runs in the cloud so the user can step away. No tab-style inline completion in the Agent surface.

inline-completion.comment: The Agent is prompt/chat driven. (Replit's separate AI features include code completion, but the Agent product itself is not tab-autocomplete.)

background-async-execution.comment: Cloud-hosted; the agent builds and tests in the background while the user does other work.

persistent-memory.comment: replit.md is always loaded into the Agent's context as ongoing project memory; checkpoints also preserve conversation context across sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Automatic checkpoints capture files, packages, config, AI conversation context, and database state; one-click bidirectional rollback/roll-forward.

git-integration.comment: Built-in Git pane with two-way GitHub sync (push commits to GitHub, pull updates), plus repo import/export.

pr-creation.comment: Two-way GitHub sync (commits/push/pull) is documented, but explicit agent-driven pull-request creation is not clearly confirmed in primary docs. Marked null.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports replit.md (always-loaded project memory) and custom_instruction/instructions.md (org-level guidance injected into the system prompt).

hooks-extensibility.comment: Third-party connectors (Stripe, OpenAI, etc.) and the ability to build agents/workflows extend behavior; no programmable event-hook API documented.

permission-model.comment: Runs in isolated cloud workspaces; the agent works conversationally with the user reviewing/accepting via checkpoints. Posture is largely autonomous within the sandbox.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Hosted SaaS on Google Cloud (US, optional India region); no on-prem/self-hosted deployment.

privacy-mode.comment: Replit states it does not train on customer code; Enterprise tier adds compliance (SOC 2 Type 2), DPA, and stronger data guarantees. Verify the specific zero-training clause for your tier.

enterprise-controls.comment: Enterprise includes SSO (SAML/OIDC), SCIM provisioning, audit logs, and granular role-based permissions. No customer-managed VPC deployment documented.

pricing-model.comment: Free Starter tier with daily Agent credits; paid subscriptions (Core/Pro) bundle monthly credits with additional usage-based agent consumption. Checked 2026-06-05.

free-tier.comment: Starter plan is free with daily Agent credits and up to 1 published project.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Replit Core: $25/mo (or $20/mo billed annually), includes $25 monthly credits. Checked 2026-06-05.

maturity.comment: Widely deployed GA product on its 4th generation with large adoption and revenue scale, though the agent capabilities still evolve rapidly.

community-size.comment: Replit has tens of millions of users overall and strong vibe-coding mindshare; the Agent is a flagship of a large platform.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Connectors marketplace/integrations (Stripe, OpenAI, etc.) and a large template ecosystem within Replit.

project-memory-files.comment: Vendor-specific replit.md is the always-loaded project memory file; org rules live in custom_instruction/instructions.md. Not the AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md conventions.

memory-scopes.comment: Per-project replit.md plus org/enterprise custom_instruction/instructions.md injected for templates. No documented per-user-global or per-folder rule scoping.

scoped-rules-files.comment: replit.md and custom_instruction/instructions.md are always applied; no documented path/glob or file-type conditional rule activation.

auto-memory.comment: Agent auto-generates/updates replit.md project memory; no documented user toggle to disable this behavior.

skill-support.comment: No documented packaged on-demand skill/slash-command mechanism for the Agent beyond instruction files and connectors. Remaining Skills attributes set null.

skill-invocation.comment: Skill Support is false; not applicable.

always-on-surface.comment: Skill Support is false; not applicable.

skill-distribution.comment: Skill Support is false; not applicable.

sandboxing-technology.comment: Code runs in hardened Linux containers with seccomp-bpf plus additional system hardening; Replit is rolling out microVMs (no shared kernel) for stronger isolation, and Agent 4 coordinates parallel tasks across isolated micro-VMs. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: Gating is coarse and tool-level: individual Agent capabilities (e.g. App Testing) can be toggled in Agent Tools, and Plan mode gates the whole build behind plan approval. No documented per-command allowlist, command-prefix rules, or per-path/per-domain user gating. Checked 2026-06-12.

rule-precedence.comment: No user-authored declarative allow/deny rule engine documented; control is via autonomy modes and tool toggles rather than precedence rules. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-configurability.comment: Users pick among predefined postures - Plan mode (approve before building), default periodic-testing mode, and Max Autonomy (Beta) - plus toggling Agent Tools. No user-authored allow/deny rules or programmable policy hooks for the agent. Checked 2026-06-12.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: Replit handles secrets architecturally - a managed Secrets store and transparent secret proxying so app code never sees raw credentials - but this is not a default agent-level deny pattern that refuses to read .env/key files in the workspace. No shipped sensitive-file deny rule documented. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: No documented mechanism to save per-command/per-action approvals that survive sessions; gating is via autonomy modes/tool toggles, not remembered allow rules. Checked 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: Max Autonomy (Beta) lets the Agent run for extended periods (200+ minutes) with minimal supervision, managing its own task list and self-supervising progress. The agent is autonomous by design within its cloud sandbox. Checked 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: Cross-application data access is blocked by default (opt-in via Connectors); MCP and connector traffic routes through a transparent proxy/sidecar that injects credentials without exposing them. Outbound network for builds is otherwise available, with access governed by these proxied, opt-in connectors. No documented hard egress block for the agent's own runs. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box the Agent writes code, installs packages, and runs/tests the app without per-action approval; Plan mode (approve-before-build) is an opt-in. Deployment specifically requires an explicit user click, but day-to-day edits and command execution run automatically. Checked 2026-06-12.

auto-accept-mode.comment: No distinct in-session 'auto-accept edits' toggle (the middle rung between per-action prompting and full bypass). The Agent already applies edits and runs commands automatically by default, so there is nothing to escape from; the available modes are Plan mode (approve-before-build, the opposite direction) and Max Autonomy/Beta (full unsupervised bypass, captured under Unsupervised Mode). Checked 2026-07-18.
